description_long: This rifle features a robust, dark metal receiver and barrel, complemented by a wooden stock and foregrip, giving it a classic military appearance. The barrel is long and cylindrical, with a ribbed section near the receiver, and is fitted with a fixed bayonet at the muzzle end, indicating its intended use for close-quarters combat as well as ranged engagements. The stock is solid wood with a smooth finish, and the foregrip is also wooden, providing ergonomic support. A detachable box magazine is visible beneath the receiver, suggesting semi-automatic or bolt-action operation. The rifle is equipped with basic iron sights for aiming, and the overall construction appears durable and utilitarian. The combination of wood and metal materials, along with the bayonet, gives the rifle a vintage, possibly mid-20th-century military look.
description: A vintage-style rifle with a wooden stock and foregrip, long metal barrel, fixed bayonet, and detachable box magazine. The design is robust and utilitarian, featuring iron sights and a classic military aesthetic.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the rifle, including the wooden stock, metal receiver, long barrel, and fixed bayonet.
description_view_1: Front-on view highlighting the narrow profile of the barrel and bayonet, with minimal detail of the stock and receiver visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, displaying the wooden stock, metal receiver, barrel, and bayonet from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the butt of the wooden stock and the alignment of the barrel and bayonet, with the magazine and trigger area less visible.
